reviews.review_text: Great location, amazing room and toilette extremely clean. Definetly reccomanded.

The lady at the office (Cindy) was really lively and very welcoming.  The park is in great location, quiet and clean. The room I stayed in was exactly what I needed and very clean and comfortable, it even had a fridge freezer, microwave, couch and a large toasting machine.  The toilets/shower was just outside my door and I had my own private undercover bbq and bbq seating area.  I definitely recommend and will be staying again in the future  ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

After reading some mixed reviews we were a little unsure. All reservations were groundless. Sandra on reception was so welcoming and although there are some permanent residents, they take such pride in their homes and we felt nothing but welcome and very safe here. Our neighbour had a beautifully cared for home and garden and we were lucky enough to meet his delightful 2 month old perfect little chihuahua. facilities immaculate. We would very highly recommend a stay here. Spacious flat site and easily walkable to waterfront restaurants etc.
Booking refused as I was "camping in a car" - it is a minivan. I've camped at several other locations with this same vehicle with no problems at all.

We recently stayed at this caravan park was very lucky to get a corner block had a lovely stay, was very quiet. Of coarse like anywhere with having some permanents you will always get the odd one, that will hoon out of the park but this can happen at any park.  Staff was lovely, & one lady that lives behind in the units lovely & friendly & helpful.  So cannot complain with our stay was very enjoyable. Don P & Wendy .
Small studio, everything you would need. Comfy, clean convenient. Handy carpark outside the front door. friendly staff. We enjoyed our stay.
Beaut chill spot, great location, good playgound for kids too

What a lovely place.
Very friendly
Cabin was well equipped for our needs
Clean and tidy.

We wanted to try a container stay and the experience here was a good one. The accommodation is clean and it comes with a small kitchenette for easy meal prep. The shared bathroom is near the container where we stayed, is safe (ladies need to enter a pin before entering) and clean with hot showers. The only drawback is there is no hair dryer provision.

Cindy and frank are the best, I have had such a pleasant stay at Mandurah coastal holiday park, the grounds are always kept clean and mowed, the staff are always nice and keen for a chat, I couldn’t be happier with my experience,

The only thing I have noticed is some of the concrete caravan slabs have cracked and deformed due to tree roots growing under the concrete, other then this very pleasant and friendly stay 😎 thanks frank and Cindy

We found the accommodation that we booked to be suitable
to our needs. It was very clean and had great linen.
We needed to book last minute and we were accommodated with no problems.
The Manager Cindy was very helpful and informative.
The location is excellent for easy access to the beach and
the town centre.
We would stay there again without hesitation.
Great stay and simple rooms, convenient location with parking.

Our stay at Mandurah Coastal Caravan Park over Easter had great, clean facilities, but we wouldn’t recommend it for families. There were a few dodgey characters around, and we felt uneasy most of the time — even management warns you to not leave valuables out.

Nous avons passé une nuit sur emplacement en Van.
Entrée autonome car arrivée tardive.
Tous s'est bien passé

Les communs sont très propres et bien entretenus.
Il y a tout le nécessaire pour la cuisine et même des denrées à partager au frigo.

We had a powered site. If this was your first time to Mandurah and you only saw this caravan park you would think this area is crap. But Mandurah is beautiful. For our last night in WA, I made a mistake booking here. So depressing. It's not close to town walking distance. No pool. There's no way I would bring kids to this caravan park. I didn't feel safe. And, considering the price, for an extra $20 to $50, I would have been able to get somewhere nicer and in town.
Great for visiting Mandurah and hunting for the Giants, staff are brilliant
I’m surprised this is a big 4 to be honest…

The Park managers were very friendly and went out their way to help us
We found ourselves in a situation of having no where to stay over the March long weekend in our Caravan, coming from SA didn’t realise the was a long weekend in WA
every place we tried was booked out right throughout the southern area.
As a last desperate thing we asked the park managers if they had any ideas of where we can go ?
They said give us a bit of time and they came back to us and showed a spot at the back of the park that isn’t normally used and offered us that if we want, obviously we took it so thank you so much
As for the park it was a great place to stay with everything we needed , close to everything and thoroughly enjoyed our stay👌👌

Stayed 2 nights in cabin with no toilet or shower. Amenities not far away.
It has towles, bedding, fridge, microwave and the kitchen has all you need.
(No stove )

We did not have a good experience with the service; there was a lack of empathy, charisma, and attention to our needs. I went to reception to make a request, and the attendant didn't let me finish speaking before responding with "no," and didn't even help me with my need. We traveled through Australia, stayed in several caravan parks, and were always very well received. This was the only one that made us feel unwelcome.

We‘ve seen a few campgrounds in WA so far and we can easily say this is the worst so far. The ground is in bad shape and needs maintenance. Lots of potholes, grass looks terrible (compared to almost every other campground). Staff not very helpful but ok. Some questionable residents, which makes the atmosphere a bit like in a US trailer park. Over all not a campground we felt comfortable and enjoyed Aussie Camping lifestyle. Kitchen and amenities are good though. Clean and well equipped. Although they use the worst access system. You get a unique code which expires and doesn’t work most of the time. You habe to enter it on a keypad which is just a black screen at first and after touching might or might not work. Why don’t make your life and the life of your guests easier and use a general code?

The park is very quiet 🤫
Great location to the city centre.
We staying 4 nights, so far no complaints, reception were welcoming. Roads were good, site a great size.
If you have an RV or big caravan 🚐 enter through Andrew Street entrance not Mandurah Terrace as we werent aware and scraped, damaging our stone guard. Quick fix by hubby and its ok again.
Very happy with our Beach Shack. Very clean, ample amenities, BBQ, outdoor area, washing line, etc. Would stay again if in Mandurah.
Would not recommend this park very rude to deal with in customer service and being lied to from a previous stay watch what they do to make people feel downgraded will not be returning

Very impressed with  our studio, sitting out area,very clean, lovely hot shower, fresh towels, shampoo, conditioner, shower gel, toilet rolls, coffee, tea, sugar, milk, washing up liquid all supplied.This is a little gem of a place

Great caravan park don’t know what others are complaining about. We had a powered site for 2 nights, with concrete slab and grass around. Some sites had a bit more shade than ours, but I’m sure if you asked for a shady site staff would oblige as they were very helpful. Amenities were spotless, yes there is a gravel road but it’s fine. There were 2 camp kitchens one with bbqs the other a fully equipped kitchen with gas stove and oven, fridge sink, kettle, toaster, microwave. Both had tvs. We had no problems with permanent residents or noise, it was actually a very quiet and peaceful park. The beach was probably a 10-15 minute walk or 5 minute bike ride. Closest caravan park to Mandurah town centre and beautiful grassy beachfront area filled with heaps of restaurants, cafes and supermarkets. Bit of a walk, or easy bike ride to town or 5 minute car ride. Bus stop just down the road if you need to get around. Excellent value for money $59 per night peak time at Christmas. Would definitely stay again
房間地理位置還不錯，床品舒適，雖然面積有點小，但是也蠻溫馨的，該有的都有，廚具也很全，淋浴間有熱水，門口有大片的草坪，總體上還算不錯。

Not the most comfortable experience.

Room is small, very small for two adults.

Complete lack of storage space, no hooks in bathroom leaving us having to throw our stuff on the damp floor or in the wet sink.

The shower and bathroom doors make such a loud squeaky I reckon' the neighbours could hear it.

They advertise free wifi, but our unit was too far from the access point making the wifi useless.

Trying to get hot water takes forever making me sad to see how much water goes to waste.

Just not our lucky stay this time unfortunately.
Great caravan park. Clean and tidy
Great
I must have liked it because I'm renting a unit

Stayed here recently, was the first time I have ever received a complementary upgrade anywhere!
Room was clean and tidy and very comfortable for my 3 day stay. Only negatives were the lack of sharp knives for cooking and the several little cockroach friends. Regardless I would absolutely stay here again and recommend to anyone. Very good value for money.

Stayed over the weekend on a grass site with a pop up camper. The site was lovely and spacious and the grass was beautiful, lush and green, coming out of winter (early November). We overlooked the playground, which was clean and fenced, perfect for our 5 year old.  The toilet block was clean and reasonably modern, with a touch pad lock. The camp kitchen was amazing, well equipped and had a tv. The location was very convenient, a short walk to restaurants at Dolphin Quay and into town. Our friends stayed in a two bedroom cabin and were very happy with it. The kitchen was well equipped and the outside lounge area was perfect for their kids, also overlooking the playground. The park is dog friendly and its a short walk to the beach and dog beach. The only downside was the area it is in. There were police sirens day and night and lots of homeless people hanging around the IGA. This is totally out of the control of the park owners/managers, but would perhaps prevent us from returning.

Room was clean, bedding was clean and comfortable. Internet did not work at all the entire time, television does not work because can’t connect to the internet.. so quite boring sitting here unable to work etc having to go to cafes BUT a good stay apart from that. Thanks for having me
Staff service was as pleasant as a covid diagnosis...site was as safe as bungee jumping with a shoelace....overall great experience

The staff have the worst attitude we had an incident where they kept our bond because apparently there was more out on the mattress in which we did not do and there was an issue with my friend beforehand who got her booking cancelled by them and never got rebooked to stay there and they kept all the money
We had cop cars in and out passing the cabin all night worst place to go if u wish to get away because it's just disgusting the way your spoken to by the staff and even more boring

Good location close to Mandurah city with clean showers and toilets. Some friendly neighbour helped during my stay, but I felt uncomfortable with one resident staring. Arrived late and had no assistance, which added some stress. Probably wouldn’t return, but it’s a best convenient spot for camping or campervans near the city. Booking is easy . And I realise there are no after hours call for assist. Not good not bad just average

No way is this a Big 4 park , my first impression  of this park was wow what a crap hole , lots of permanents  scattered throughout  the  park , broken cement slabs , pot holes , but to be expected  with all the rain , good spots for caravans scattered  here and there throughout  the park , looks like they might be trying to upgrade ,nice little  kids playground  , a nice camp kitchen  , but what really caught me by surprise  , was how good the toilet and laundry  bloke are , very clean,  modernish,  they both have a touch panel on them which I found impressive  for the look of the park ,if they upgraded  , could  be a nice park , young lady at reception  was very nice and helpful  , all in all,  if you do free camping , park not so bad with another look
有野外渡假感覺但又不失方便。

Booked a cabin with complete amenities. Spacious and clean. Friendly staff at the counter with easy check in and out. They charged a deposit of 250AUD, which is quite high for one night stay, but received back the deposit a few hours after checked out.
The smallest accommodation I've ever stayed in. To the point of  claustrophobic. I'm not that tall and couldn't fit on the bed.was definitely not worth the moneyeven as budget accommodation.

Was a great little room for the price. The beach shack offered outside lounge and TV with ample utilities in the kitchen. Everything was super clean and staff wonderful. Would love to go back sometime.
Very happy with the service given to me and my family while we stayed there
Great stay for one night. Shower was awesome.

There was no hot water in the shower and we couldn’t take a bath. There was no heater in the room either . It was quite small for 2. We couldn’t change a room as the reception closes after 6 pm. You also have to throw your own trash and ensure you do not leave a big mess or the security deposit will be forfeited $100 for cleaning.

Stayed in Family Cabin for a night heading more south. Cabins very spacious has everything you need to cook for families rooms are spacious clean and super comfy after 8 days holidaying one of our best nights sleep absolutely amazing. Wish we had longer. Only reated 4 star for service as we weren't there long enough as we arrived late and left on check out but lady in office was a delight x
Clean ,close totown

I won’t rush back and there are much better parks around
Firstly there is an unofficial ruler of the park: a long-term resident who spends her days glaring at guests like she’s personally offended by your presence. You’ll know her — she’s the one with the permanent scowl and the air of someone who’s been elected mayor of the caravan park in her own mind. Watching people seems to be her full-time hobby, and she does it with such smug satisfaction you’d think she was being paid for it.

Fellow residents just laugh it off — apparently this woman has a long-standing reputation for being the local fun police. Honestly, it’s impressive how one person can drag down the atmosphere of an entire park just by existing near other humans.

If you want to stay here, you will have to get used to regular Police visits they seem to have an ongoing unhealthy relationship with the park, and generally seeing a few unsightly comings and goings

The good bit…leaving

Until then, bring earplugs, patience, and maybe a priest to perform an exorcism

Cleaners need to learn how to clean… in a fairly new family cabin and floors dirty, drains dirty, kids hand prints all over the glass entry door and light fittings, mould, leaking shower from the fixture, leaking kettle, piss hair and dust in base of toilets, food splashed up walls and in the oven, air con doesn’t work properly, floor drains lead straight to dirt letting bugs into the cabin.

Manager had cleaners come through and fix some of the issues but overall to have a team clean one check and then have to send a third round out and couldn’t even make the place clean is disappointing. Couldn’t stay anywhere else as last places were full.

Internal roads full of pot holes and laneways near the new cabins are super tight if you have a medium to large car.

Would not recommend unless desperate… until they atleast attempt to fix the place. Not disgruntled with management the lady I felt with tried but staff need training.

Good location, we were at the back of the park so it was nice and quiet,

Please read full review before you decide:|I’ve stayed here a few times so I wasn’t as shocked as my family when we drove in. |Potholed roads and old cabins are your first view, followed by large vacant building sites cordoned off with flags, and some dubious looking locals using the through-road from neighbouring streets. It looks hideous right now! |They are in the process of installing heaps of brand new cabins which are to the side, but it looks terrible atm (July 2025)|I calmed the farm and parked next to our 2 bedroom cabin. Upon entering they all realised it was ok. Inside was spacious and spotless with a great kitchen, 3 aircons, 2 TVs and 2 bathrooms. What more can you expect for the price? (Ok, so we’d really like a couple of clothes hanging hooks or shelves. There are none, zip, zero. Plenty of places for them though. |The cleaners do a great job here, showers are hot, beds are comfy and the living area is nice with a 6 seater table and L lounge. |Full size fridge, oven/stove and microwave. |So….. we’d all stay here again without a doubt. Just use it as a base to explore the area and come back here to cook dinner, have a beer and sleep. Do that and you’ll get great value for the money. |If you need a resort style place with pools, restaurants etc this isn’t for you.
In the process  of new park managers. They have a big job getting  the patk to a better standard
Caravan site

Stayed in cabin 1 which was right next to the road, very noisy, managers gate banged every night.
1st morning found skid marks on the doona and floors were very sticky. Cleaner came back and changed bed linen. Didn't wash the floors. The second doona had stain marks and small amount of blood on the clean sheet. Manager herself came and changed the bed again. I washed the floors to make them walkable.
Next morning after rain, had a leak in the lounge that dripped onto the power point. Manager gave us some towels. That was fixed after the weekend. Later that morning after more rain water poured into the closed windows in the kitchen, bedroom and lounge. Kitchen ended up flooded and bedroom was soaked, with some of our belongings also soaked. Thanks to Patty who finally shifted us to an empty cabin for the last 2 days of our stay. We couldn't move before as it was a long weekend and nowhere in Mandurah had accommodation.
Would never stay there again.
The park needs work.

New owners / manager
Didn't deliver what I expected
I'm sure they can do better with a little more experience and knowledge of the park.
Excellent facility,  great location and service 👍

I have stayed here heaps of times and I like the place. Close to the pub, beach, cafes a short walk, iga around the corner. Good priced rooms. You will need to book ahead for events and school holidays.
I had a fantastic stay at mandurah coastal holiday park.

A quiet park close to everything. Walking distance to beach and local shops. Bike tracks and bus stops.|Amenities a little dated but always very clean.|Can see new owners working on updates. Very friendly. Had a nice shady spot. Stayed for 2 weeks.

Caravanning..
Good sized sites w cement pad, green grass, clean, secure abolition blocks. A bit squeeze coming in w big van.  Nice n quiet. Good, friendly service at front desk & also caretaker lovely.
Close to shops, all amenities.
Will return.

The room was compact but cozy , furnishing was well done despite the small size . Only microwave available to cook your meals . Check in was a bit of hassle as they require the card details before checking u else no late arrivals. Luckily staff was responsive and provide good assistance
Value for money, room was clean and service was good.

Older style park that is very central, lovely grassed areas, amenities & park were very clean, a little tight for some bigger vans so maybe check with management as a few I spoke with had trouble getting in. Also had a bloke wandering around late at night snooping under awnings etc so make sure locked up ..

I highly recommend this holiday park which is situated close to Mandurah town.   The common areas, toilets and camp kitchen are super clean.  Staff are welcoming and there is a lovely bbq area.   Thank you to Ann and her team (particularly Shaz).   Great couple of nights here!!
Lovely park but a tight squeeze for larger vans over 22ft
Clean facilities and good caravan site.

We camped here a year ago ( Labour Day WA 2024) and it was okay & reasonably quiet considering the location.
Awesome spot very close to the dog beach near Silver Sands (under1km walking distance) and Dolphin Quay with all the Mandurah activities.
Back then the amenities were quite old, pretty basic & in need of maintenance/upgrades.
What a difference 12 months can make. New owners/managers with a lot more willingness to make the park a better place for visitors.
Lots of things have been tidied up, painted, cleaned and improved.
We also noticed some positive changes in the client base. Family + dog-friendly campsite.
They also have serviced cabins (but obviously no pets).

I can highly recommend this little caravan park. My mother and myself stayed one night in a chalet. Even though it was older looking the interior was lovely. The beds were fresh and new and the linen was 5 star. For a long standing park the owners have done a beautiful job. Thanks for a lovely stay.
Place very near n easy to go to mandurah cruise. So clean n privacy place. Very recommended.

This caravan park is the worst I've stated in apart from the poor facilities I've travelled all over Australia. The couple that run it are the rudest, unfriendly, aggressive people I've ever dealt with. Everyone I spoke with there had the same opinion and actually moved to another park as I am. People like them shouldn't be in that industry.
John Stokes.
Really appreciate the very friendly & helpful staff.
Wonderful getaway place.  Good service, clean rooms and friendly neighbour's.

Location and availability is all this park has going for it.  Hospitality from management is lacking greatly. The owner seemed nice enough , saw her once in almost 4 weeks , but the manager was not approachable, welcoming or friendly,  she was downright rude to me on my first day . If we had any other choice we would not of stayed as long as we did . We moved parks as soon as a site was available.  Management should try showering there , all showers the pressure is all over the place.  One second it's freezing,  next it's hot or it barely flows . 1 key for all amenities per site is not fun  . Bins not emptied regularly or not returned to places around the park and stockpiled at the back , not a once off occurrence. Speed was not adhered to and never once saw it enforced. Our site had a lot of smoke butts on it , which i picked up .
Cleaners did a good job of keeping amenities clean and were friendly , management should of had them in twice a day or checked themselves ,as when park was busy  it quickly got dirty and ran out of tp later in the day . I can appreciate it's a busy time of year , but if you are happy to take peoples money you should provide extra services to cover the extra occupants . Playground is minimal with no shade and no jumping pillow like shown on website .
Location was really great , beach a short nice walk , iga , bakery , chemist, service station,  massage,  nail salon and a pub all within walking distance 😀
我觉得贼棒！！！这个价格可以住到这种真的太棒了！！！我觉得每一次来这里，我都会住这家。而且特别适合一家庭的那种。
Pelican Lodge was a great cabin for us with an air conditioner in each area. Very comfortable for 2 couples, each with our own bathroom.

Terrible communication from staff, we called to let them know we would be checking in out of hours and followed their protocols. We were never sent any codes or OOH contact numbers. I had to interrupt another guest to get a contact number. When I called, I was provided with the wrong code twice, whilstsomeone condescendingly told me I was putting in the code wrong over the phone. No apologies were given for lack of communication. No WiFi details were given. TV wouldn't work without WiFi, so no point in having it. Pictures taken are using a fish eye lens, so we were disappointed by room size, at least everything was clean. Curtain is not big enough, so people can see in at an angle if they wanted... Even on check out, nobody at reception ~09:30 (hours 9-6) so we called again, and were rudely told to use the key drop box, even though it's labelled as out of hours key drop. Spent the last month using various Holiday/Campervan Parks, and this was by far the worst check-in experience.
Had a great stay. Warmly greeted on arrival at reception. Caravan park kept neat & tidy, ammenities clean, staff very friendly.

The place itself its good . But for some of the bigger caravan 20ft to 24 ft .it's can be very tight
We were on site 42 , the ground was uneven,  I found it very tight and complicated

Phoned up for uncovered tent site, which they have advertised. Had to have powered site. Wanted $69 for the night, paid half that at Monkey Mia Park which has a 5 star bush kitchen set-up. Not impressed again. WA, the Underwhelming State.
So rude on the phone that I chose to stay elsewhere.

Caravan slab booked and cabin

Lovely quiet camp
Great staff
New management lots of great changes

The location makes this park one to stay. Staff were not really friendly, I know it is a busy time, but it does help if you do stop and have a quick chat. Times for cleaning are not adhered to, which makes leaving by 10am. very difficult. Bins were overflowing regularly. Rules are different than other parks. I've never heard that swags,tents can only stay 1 week, then they have to leave for 1 week. Prices are ridiculous, even Xmas, for what is offering.
Nice little cabins service excellent

Terrible communication from staff, we called to let them know we would be checking in out of hours and followed their protocols. We were never sent any codes or OOH contact numbers. I had to interrupt another guest to get a contact number. When I called, I was provided with the wrong code twice, whilst
someone condescendingly told me I was putting in the code wrong over the phone. No apologies were given for lack of communication. No WiFi details were given. TV wouldn't work without WiFi, so no point in having it. Pictures taken are using a fish eye lens, so we were disappointed by room size, at least everything was clean. Curtain is not big enough, so people can see in at an angle if they wanted... Even on check out, nobody at reception ~09:30 (hours 9-6) so we called again, and were rudely told to use the key drop box, even though it's labelled as out of hours key drop. Spent the last month using various Holiday/Campervan Parks, and this was by far the worst check-in experience.

We needed some accomodation at short notice and all the hofels were booked out. |We rented a Family deluxe cabin and a Motel room. |Both were exceptionally clean, tidy and fresh inside. Faultless actually!|There are so many options here including camp, caravan sites, beach shacks, motel rooms, donga style, family suites and more.|Value is superb.|Walk to the shops, beach, Silver Sands etc.|Highly recomend this one.
Clean, neat and tidy and honestly the booking doesn't do it justice to how good it is in person

Great location, clean perfect room sizes, friendly and prompt response.
🙂 Depending on your preference one bedroom cabin for hubby and I had kitchen, bathroom dining table, couch, aircon, hot water jug, microwave, toaster, cutlery, cups etc.   towels provided, shower had soap, shampoo, conditioner and shower gel.   I love it.

Great location, short walk to shops, beach and Marina. The BBQ area and kitchen were of a high standard and so nice to have clean cloths, detergent and gas stove in the kitchen.   Clean very good amenities.  Nice looking fenced kids playground. They also have cabins/shacks. Renovations to some accommodation underway when we were there. Only mild annoyance was one key for amenities rather than code entry.
Lovely holiday park. Short walk into town and the beach (took about 20 mins)
hot water not working, expensive for what it offers

Place was dirty not clean my parents paid top dollar to stay for my neice 18th bday and graduation and stayed here.room smelt like mould.aircon turned on but heater was just just cool air then could  not turn it off.toilet button were dark marks on it.bathroom door dirty.my folks woke up with crazy headaches.finally spoke to the lady about it and she wasn’t happy about them reporting it to her won’t stay again will recommend staying eles were for that sort of money.said she will have a look and that is all that was offered..no stars here also was told if room was left dirty will be charged automatically out of our cards $100.00 dollars for cleaning the room.

Stayed one night |Clean |Comfy |Friendly |Tea coffee supplied |Good location |Shampoo supplied |Only downfall was spotlight out front of room was so bright all night curtain didn’t save it|Nice little accomm for short stay |Reception staff lovely and friendly

This Caravan Park was given 3 stars, because it is a work in progress. The studio accommodation we stayed in was very clean, looked like some renovation work had been done on it. A little on the small side but had everything needed for a short stay. The new owners have only had it for little over a year but you can see improvements have been happening. Pricing was good.

Don’t go to this park it may look ok but there are residents who live there who get around in the night terrorising other residents and visitors. They theive and damage property . This has all been reported to management by residents and visitors and nothing is done about it continues. Think twice !!!
Host at reception was very rude,lock everything away ,won't suggest staying there

Tiny double bed room, private bathroom (hairdryer) fridge, microwave, cutlery, plates, cup, table and two chairs.
nice place
Clean. Really clean. Friendly
Small motel room but good shower and very comfy bed. Worked well for a one night stay on a road trip!

Worst experience ever had no towels in room after check in , check in also wasent until 2pm and after travelling 3 hours with a 2 and half old newborn he was due for feeding soon the staff was overall rude and no help with anything , not just that they had known I had a newborn and when asked for and extra towel for him they told me I’ve got too many people in my room when it was stated it was for and infant and still that did not change the outcome even then knowing it was free of charge for and infant but then came up with the excuse you only get 2 towels supplied for your stay in total of 7 days fir 2 people which is also really unhygienic considering the place was riddled with mold and they would of known that
Great modern well appointed cabin and very cheap
Beau camping, près de la plage ! Accueil très chaleureux. Sanitaire et cuisine très propre.

We booked 3 nights on a powered site. Many of the barbecues are taped off …swings taped off …packed in like sardines . Caravans are so packed together that you could hear the conversations in the next van. Our caravan was next to a permanent and she smoked all day long and then when she had finished she just threw the butt on to the ground …disgusting…no respect. The road noise was s really annoying. Won’t stay again unless they spend some money and improve the facilities.

Great location close to boardwalk facilities, beach and shops. |Very clean and well kept toilets, showers and laundry. |Great bbq area. |Loved the friendly staff and long term residents. |Felt very safe. |Myself and others had belongings left out overnight without issue. |Park is doing ongoing upgrades and improvements and it shows. |Would recommend.

We stayed for a week in our caravan in Mandurah coastal caravan park. The second night my husband's bike was stolen.  Another property on site was entered and private material stolen. The site's security cameras have been given to the police together with our statement. Apparently this is an ongoing problem on this site although at no time were we advised of same, nor were any fellow campers. More recently my son has discovered two cards were removed from his car on site and used for transactions in the area on the same date.
It's not on the coast by any means

Highly recommend staying in this caravan park, the staff were super friendly on the phone and in person. They took over the park 5-6 months ago and have made significant changes, that we noticed.
- 2 x security driving past at night (for our safety)
- residents are taught the new rules, for their safety as well
- looks like the bathrooms have been recently upgraded
- entrances are closed to stop a thoroughfare.
we LOVED staying here and the residents that we met were SUPER lovely. We will be back any time we are in the area.

Lots of permanent residents here. Looked like we are the only camper here.  Amenities are super clean and kitchen too, but unfortunately we didn’t use it. You will get a key to open the toilets shower rooms and kitchen.

Concrete and grass make me so comfortable.
I don’t need to worry about bringing sand to my Caravan.

I llke the park but not everyone's cup of tea. Lots of permanents but friendly enough if you take the time to say G'day. They have had a problem with thieves but are installing CCTV. Toilets and showers are clean. Location is close to shopping centre and pub. If you are up for a walk there is plenty to see. Will stay again

Great spot, level concrete  sites
Amenities clean, staff friendly
Just spent 5 nights and will go back again thankyou

The Staff are lovely and as accommodating as possible and location is good (takes about 10 mins to walk to beach)
- but unfortunately the removal of the Jumping pillow made it a bit of a downer for us.

The permanent residents weren't very friendly and made us feel as though we were imposing... Also the need to put everything inside our cars at night due to constant theft makes it less desirable when travelling with our young family.

That being said we would stay again due to the experience with the staff 👌

Just been there for a week friends got their bike stolen on the first night people next to us got bike stolen on third night and we had two bags taken out of the car dumped in the local park not safe for kids or family locals scouting out your stuff during the day right in front of you owners and police not too worried left a day early please stay away for your Safty
Lovely park, clean amenities, friendly staff. Would stay there again

I really wanted to love this place. Staff very welcoming and friendly, nice grassy sites, perfect location and great amenities..... But...we had our site scavenged every night and random things stolen each night. We stayed 4 nights.

- empty bike bag, attached to our bike. Bikes were knocked over and twisted. If they weren't chained together, they would have been taken
- a small flashlight in the side pocket of our outside table
- a spare bike tyre tube attached the the bike
- a gazebo wall
-.one night we left outside lights on and they turned them off!!
-one night we didn't notice anything taken, but a neighbour told us they saw someone looking around our site around 4:30am.

Staff is aware. We saw security cameras being installed at the beginning of our visit, and then during the day and early evening we saw police/security presence. But it obviously hasn't helped.

Staff say gates are going to be installed. Not sure when.

If you stay, secure every tiny thing. Who steals a gazebo wall?
Lovely friendly helpful staff. Always helped with special needs. camp kitchen is awesome! Toilets etc very clean. Close walking distance to everything. cannot fault ❤️

If you can’t stay anywhere else this should only be a last resort.  Amenities are clean, property, concrete slabs, roads, grass - tired and need lots of works & TLC.  Can’t say we felt overly safe, lots of permanent sites (no disrespect to these folks). Asked for shade and pic below displays… for $50 a night a totally rip off.  My first time leaving a negative review - admin staff could improve by going out at different times of the day to know when & where there is shade.  Also move bins so they don’t impact access and egress.
New owner, very dated,needs refurbishment

Just visited family.
Very nice park.
The site is beautiful and clean and the staff are friendly and extremely helpful. We highly recommend this park to anyone.
